sourceMovie.title: Gattaca
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Gattaca for the same reason it broke through in 1997: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Andrew Niccol keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What Happened to Monday
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gattaca clicked for you, What Happened to Monday (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gattaca clicked for you, Pandorum (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gattaca?
faq.answer: Try What Happened to Monday, Pandorum, The X-Files—each shares DNA with Gattaca while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gattaca?
faq.answer: Gattaca (1997) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction, Mystery, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gattaca worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
